kept.text: Free accounts for teachers, 50% off for students!

profile_bio.description: ceo @replit. civilizationist

quoted_tweet.text:
The classroom changed. The homework changed. How we learn changed.

18 million students already build on Replit and now, there's a dedicated space just for them. No setup. No lectures. Just building.

Teachers get free access. Students get 50% off Core. And if you're running a program, we'll help you scale it.

Claim yours at https://t.co/BZ8lLCz08M

profile_bio.description: Idea to app, fast. Create beautiful, modern web applications at the speed of thought with the power of Replit's AI Agent.

kept.text:
The easiest way to get started learning to use AI is to start with Replit. Just go there, sign up and build something. Anything. Even just a presentation. If you don't know how to do it, just ask it how. Just talk with it. No instruction manual necessary.

After that, if you want to build local apps, by far the best approach right now, which costs almost nothing, is to use DeepSeek V4 Pro together with an OpenCode harness. If you don't know how to set that up, ask your geeky computer expert friend and give them this post. And they'll set it up.

What can you build with it? Anything you can image. Last weekend I built a book cover image translator that's now in production. If you can imagine it, you can build it.

If you wanna become pro at AI, this post is for you

- for writing don't use ChatGPT, use Claude

- for research don't use Google, use Grok

- for presentation don't use Canva, use Gamma

- for building apps don't use Replit, use Claude code

- for generating images don't use ChatGPT, use Nano banana

- for prompting don't type, use Wisper flow

- for agentic automation don't use N8N, use OpenClaw

Introducing Replit Slides!

Made an entire pitch deck in under a minute without touching a single slide. Just describe what you want, chat to iterate, and export to PowerPoint, Google Slides, or PDF (or publish a live URL). Slide Decks are available to Core and Pro users. 🎯 https://t.co/Qe2f6y8g9c

profile_bio.description: Idea to app, fast. Create beautiful, modern web applications at the speed of thought with the power of Replit's AI Agent.

kept.text:
i vibe coded an app that predicted a landslide in my own village 4 days before it happened 

No, im not a geologist

A few weeks ago I built a flood relief platform for Dagestan with Replit. 

The idea was simple: people affected by floods could post what they needed, and people who wanted to help could find them.

It went viral.

Millions of views. 15,000+ reposts. I won the Replit Agent 4 contest, got featured on Replit’s pages, and Amjad even mentioned it, i even got mentioned in federal hearings by a senator by NAME.

I won’t lie, that felt unreal.

So I kept building.

I added a command center: weather maps, 40+ parameters, news monitoring, AI matching between victims and helpers.

Then I found NASA material on landslide prediction.

I’m not a geologist, but I put the course, model logic, and data sources into NotebookLM, studied it, and built a version using the NASA approach.

Then I connected it to a Telegram bot.

A few days later, I got an alert:

70% landslide risk. In my own village.

The model saw rapid temperature change in the mountains, then combined it with slope, terrain roughness, soil moisture, satellite data, and historical patterns.

I texted my relatives. I posted about it. Most people ignored it.

4 days later, a major landslide hit the village.

This is the craziest thing I’ve ever built. Not because I’m some genius.

Because I’m literally just a guy from Dagestan using Replit, Claude Code, NotebookLM, and public NASA data.

This kind of power is now available to regular people.

That still blows my mind. You can just build things! 

Replit, you created a monster 🤣
